title: allocasuarina muelleriana subsp. alticola
intro: Allocasuarina muelleriana subsp. alticola, also known as Alpine Sheoak, is a subspecies of tree in the family Casuarinaceae native to Victoria, Australia. The plant has been used in traditional Aboriginal medicine for its medicinal properties, including antibacterial and anti-inflammatory activities, which are attributed to the presence of compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Allocasuarina muelleriana subsp. alticola include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its antimicrobial properties, which can help to prevent infections. The herbal preparations of this plant are typically made from its leaves, twigs, and bark, which can be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures.
